{"step_by_step_thinking": "The bicarbonate/carbon dioxide (HCO3/H2CO3) buffer system is crucial for maintaining the pH of the human body. The key aspect of this buffer system is its ability to accept and donate hydrogen ions (H+). This is evident from the equations provided in the documents, such as H+ + HCO3 <--> H2CO3 <--> CO2 + H2O. This system works by shifting the equilibrium to counteract changes in pH. The pKa of the bicarbonate buffer system is approximately 6.1, which is close to the physiological pH of 7.4. While the components can be increased or decreased in the body, and it is a combination of a weak acid and a weak base, the primary reason for its effectiveness as a buffer is its ability to accept and donate H+ ions.", "answer_choice": "C"}
